description: Phase 4 — DDGC Condition Closure, Damage Variance, and Dungeon Fidelity. Close B-004 by implementing remaining DDGC conditions (HP-threshold, InMode, OnKill), activate rolled damage policy, and align dungeon map generation with DDGC's original MapGenerator parameters.

userStories.title: P4a-0: Inventory remaining condition touchpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a precise inventory of which migrated DDGC skills and statuses depend on unimplemented condition variants so Phase 4a targets only the conditions with real migrated content.
acceptanceCriteria: A checked-in inventory file (e.g., tasks/phase4_condition_inventory.md) lists every migrated skill/status that references a condition not yet implemented in ConditionAdapter
The inventory maps each unimplemented condition to the skills that require it, grouped by condition family (HP-threshold, dungeon-mode, kill-trigger, etc.)
At least 2 condition families are selected as implementation anchors with concrete skill anchor names
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Discovery slice. Scan src/content/heroes/ and src/content/monsters/ for .with_game_condition() calls referencing unimplemented tags. Also scan for ddgc_ prefixed condition strings in skill definitions.

userStories.title: P4a-1: Implement HP-threshold conditions in ConditionAdapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need HpAbove, TargetHpAbove, and TargetHpBelow DDGC conditions implemented in ConditionAdapter so HP-threshold skill gating works correctly instead of being always-on or always-off.
acceptanceCriteria: ConditionAdapter::evaluate_ddgc() handles ddgc_hp_above_<threshold>, ddgc_target_hp_above_<threshold>, and ddgc_target_hp_below_<threshold> tags
HP-threshold evaluation reads ATTR_HEALTH and ATTR_MAX_HEALTH from the actor or target in ConditionContext, computing fractional HP as health/max_health, and compares against the threshold
At least one fixture skill (e.g., hp_above_strike) is added to src/content/skills.rs or a test fixture that uses an HP-threshold condition via .with_game_condition()
Focused tests prove: (a) condition passes when HP fraction is above threshold, (b) fails when below threshold, (c) boundary at exact threshold is deterministic
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md B-004 notes are updated to mention HP-threshold conditions as implemented
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: ConditionContext already exposes actors HashMap. Read ATTR_HEALTH and ATTR_MAX_HEALTH attributes directly. Threshold is fractional (0.0-1.0), e.g., ddgc_hp_above_0.5 means HP > 50% of max.

userStories.title: P4a-2: Implement InMode dungeon condition in ConditionAdapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the InMode DDGC condition implemented so skills that behave differently in specific dungeon modes can diverge correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: ConditionAdapter::evaluate_ddgc() handles ddgc_in_mode_<mode> tags
ConditionContext gains an optional mode field (String or enum) that can be set alongside the existing dungeon field
Mode resolution reads from ConditionContext::dungeon (already present) and the new mode field
At least one fixture skill uses an InMode condition via .with_game_condition()
Focused test proves: (a) condition passes when current dungeon/mode matches the tag, (b) fails when dungeon/mode does not match
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md B-004 notes are updated to mention InMode as implemented
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DDGC's InMode is tied to dungeon-specific battle modifiers (e.g., in_black_tortoise_field). The ConditionContext already carries dungeon: Dungeon. Add mode: Option<String> field. Mode is set by the encounter resolver when entering a boss room with a battle modifier.

userStories.title: P4a-3: Implement OnKill condition with KillTracker
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an OnKill DDGC condition implemented so skills that trigger bonus effects on enemy death can execute correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: A KillTracker struct is added to src/run/ (similar pattern to UsageCounters in src/run/usage_counters.rs)
KillTracker records which actors killed an enemy on the current turn, reset at end of turn
ConditionAdapter::evaluate_ddgc() handles ddgc_on_kill tags by checking KillTracker for the current actor
ConditionContext gains access to KillTracker data (either direct reference or through the thread-local pattern)
At least one fixture skill uses the OnKill condition via .with_game_condition()
Focused test proves: (a) condition passes on a turn where the actor killed an enemy, (b) fails when no kill occurred this turn
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: KillTracker needs: record_kill(actor_id, target_id) called after a combat resolution results in target death, reset_turn() called at end of turn, actor_killed_this_turn(actor_id) -> bool for condition evaluation.

userStories.title: P4a-4: Close B-004 condition blocker and update documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the condition system's coverage documented and B-004 fully resolved so no further condition work is tracked as a blocker.
acceptanceCriteria: SEMANTIC_GAPS.md SG-002 (BuffRule condition downgrade) is updated: classification changed from partially resolved to resolved, description updated to list all implemented DDGC conditions
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md B-004 status is changed from 'Partially resolved' to 'Resolved'

A condition coverage table in SEMANTIC_GAPS.md or a dedicated file lists: (a) all implemented DDGC conditions (9+), (b) original 35+ variants, (c) remaining unimplemented conditions classified as 'low-impact deferred' or 'not used in migrated content'
cargo test passes with all condition implementations active
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Closeout slice. After US-801 through US-804, the ConditionAdapter covers: FirstRound, StressAbove, StressBelow, DeathsDoor, TargetHasStatus, ActorHasStatus, HpAbove, TargetHpAbove, TargetHpBelow, InMode, OnKill = 11 DDGC conditions plus 3 framework-native bridges.

userStories.title: P4b-0: Add DamageRange registry for migrated skill definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a registry that maps skill IDs to their DDGC DamageRange (min/max) so the damage policy can choose between fixed and rolled modes without changing skill definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: A SkillDamageRegistry struct exists in src/run/ (or src/run/damage_policy.rs) that maps skill_id: &str -> DamageRange
SkillDamageRegistry is populated with DamageRange entries for at least 10 migrated skills (mix of hero and monster skills)
SkillDamageRegistry min/max values are verified against DDGC source data (e.g., crusading_strike 8-15, rend 4-8 from PHASE2_CONDITION_HITPOLICY_INVENTORY.md)
Existing EffectNode::damage(average) calls in skill definitions are NOT changed — registry is parallel/additive
Focused test proves: (a) registry lookup returns correct DamageRange, (b) missing skill ID returns a fallback (fixed DamageRange from average), (c) min <= max for all entries
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Do NOT change EffectNode::damage() calls. Add a parallel SkillDamageRegistry keyed by skill ID. The registry stores DamageRange::new(min, max) from DDGC data. When a skill ID is not in the registry, use DamageRange::fixed(current_average_value) as fallback.

userStories.title: P4b-1: Wire DamagePolicy into EncounterResolver battle loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the EncounterResolver to accept a DamagePolicy parameter so the battle loop can resolve damage using either FixedAverage or Rolled mode.
acceptanceCriteria: EncounterResolver gains a damage_policy field of type DamagePolicy (default: FixedAverage for backward compatibility)
When resolving a damage effect, the battle loop queries SkillDamageRegistry for the skill's DamageRange and applies the active DamagePolicy to compute the resolved damage value
When DamagePolicy::FixedAverage is active, all damage values match current behavior (existing tests pass unchanged)
When DamagePolicy::Rolled is active, each damage effect resolves to a value within [min, max] using a seeded RNG
A deterministic test using a fixed seed proves Rolled damage stays within range bounds for at least 3 different skills
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The EncounterResolver already uses a seeded approach for encounter selection. Extend the RNG or seed to cover damage rolls. DamagePolicy::Rolled uses DamageRange::roll(&mut rng). All existing tests use FixedAverage by default — no behavior change.

userStories.title: P4b-2: Update damage variance documentation and close B-006
userStories.description: As a developer, I need SG-001 and B-006 updated to reflect that damage variance is now available so the semantic gap ledger is accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: SEMANTIC_GAPS.md SG-001 classification is updated from 'Acceptable approximation' to 'Resolved — Rolled policy available; FixedAverage remains default for deterministic testing'
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md B-006 notes are updated to mention SkillDamageRegistry and DamagePolicy wiring
cargo test passes with both FixedAverage (default) and a smoke test that exercises Rolled mode
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Closeout slice for B-006. The Rolled policy is now available but FixedAverage remains the default for test stability.

userStories.title: P4c-0: Define DungeonMapConfig struct and populate from MapGenerator.txt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a DungeonMapConfig struct with DDGC map generation parameters so each dungeon generates maps matching the original's structure.
acceptanceCriteria: A DungeonMapConfig struct exists in src/run/ (or src/run/dungeon_config.rs) with fields: base_room_number, base_corridor_number, gridsize (rows, cols), connectivity (f64), min_final_distance, hallway_battle (min, max), hallway_curio (min, max), hallway_hunger (min, max), total_room_battles (min, max), room_guarded_curio (min, max), room_guarded_treasure (min, max)
DungeonSize enum exists: Short, Medium
A function dungeon_map_config(dungeon: Dungeon, size: DungeonSize) -> DungeonMapConfig returns the correct config for each combination
4 dungeon configs x 2 sizes = 8 DungeonMapConfig instances are defined with values extracted from MapGenerator.txt in DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Mechanics/
Focused test proves: (a) QingLong short has base_room_number=9, (b) BaiHu connectivity=0.85, (c) ZhuQue connectivity=0.95, (d) medium configs have more rooms than short configs
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Data-model slice only. Do not integrate with DefaultRoomGenerator yet. Read values directly from MapGenerator.txt source file. Key values: QingLong short=9 rooms/10 corridors/4x3 grid/0.9 connectivity; BaiHu short=9/10/5x4/0.85; ZhuQue short=9/10/5x5/0.95; XuanWu short=9/10/6x3/0.9.

userStories.title: P4c-1: Wire DungeonMapConfig into floor generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need DungeonMapConfig parameters fed into the framework's room/floor generation so generated maps reflect DDGC's dungeon structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Floor creation or Run initialization reads the appropriate DungeonMapConfig for the current dungeon and size
Room generation uses per-dungeon room count, corridor count, connectivity, and grid size from the config
A deterministic test proves: (a) QingLong short maps have 9 rooms, (b) BaiHu maps have lower connectivity than ZhuQue maps, (c) short vs medium maps differ in room count (9 vs 14)
Existing run slices continue passing (backward compatibility with generic room generation as fallback)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The framework's DefaultRoomGenerator or Floor generation needs to accept these parameters. If the framework API doesn't support custom configs, implement a DDGC-specific room generator that delegates to the framework with configured parameters. Prefer configuration over framework patches.

userStories.title: P4c-2: Parse DDGC encounter pack weights from dungeon .bytes files
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the encounter pack weights from DDGC dungeon .bytes files available at runtime so monster composition in rooms matches the original game.
acceptanceCriteria: A DungeonEncounterConfig struct captures the weighted encounter definitions from dungeon .bytes files (mash blocks with hall/room/boss lines and .chance/.types fields)
At least one dungeon (QingLong) has fully parsed encounter data from QingLong.bytes matching its mash block structure
Each parsed encounter pack is registered in the existing EncounterPackRegistry with correct monster IDs and chance weights
Focused test proves encounter selection produces the expected monster composition for a given seed (e.g., seed=0 selects a specific pack)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The dungeon .bytes files are text format (not binary despite extension). They use mash: blocks with hall:/room:/boss: lines. Each line has .chance N and .types monster_id1 monster_id2... Parse into the existing EncounterPackRegistry. Start with QingLong.bytes as the anchor.

userStories.title: P4c-3: Validate dungeon fidelity end-to-end with DDGC configs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an end-to-end run slice that uses DDGC-configured dungeon maps and encounter packs so the full Phase 4c integration is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: A run slice using a DDGC dungeon (QingLong short) completes successfully with correct room count (9), correct encounter types, and correct monster families per room
The run trace records dungeon type, map parameters (room count, corridor count), and encounter composition per room
Existing run slices (e.g., run_ddgc_slice) continue passing without modification (backward compatibility)
cargo test passes with DDGC dungeon configs active in the new run slice
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Integration validation slice. This is the Phase 4c closeout. Add a new scenario or run slice function (e.g., run_qinglong_short_slice) that exercises the DDGC-configured dungeon path. Do not modify existing run slices.
